# Command reference

<!-- Generated by tools/generate.ts — edit the source, not this file. -->

Every edit is one of these 58 serializable commands, dispatched through
`engine.dispatch({ type, ...payload })`, `mcut apply`, or the matching MCP tool.
Payloads are zod-validated; invalid commands throw without changing the project.
For a full JSON schema: `mcut commands --name <command>`.

## addTrack

Add a new track. Tracks later in the list render on top.

- `id` (string, optional)
- `name` (string, optional)
- `index` (integer, optional)

## removeTrack

Remove a track and all elements on it.

- `trackId` (string, required)

## renameTrack

Rename a track.

- `trackId` (string, required)
- `name` (string, required)

## setTrackFlags

Mute (audio), hide (visuals), lock, or magnetically compact a track.

- `trackId` (string, required)
- `muted` (boolean, optional)
- `hidden` (boolean, optional)
- `locked` (boolean, optional)
- `magnetic` (boolean, optional)

## compactTrackGaps

Close every gap on one track by packing clips left in timeline order.

- `trackId` (string, required)

## compactTimelineGaps

Close every gap on the timeline by packing clips left on every track.

## reorderTrack

Move a track to a new position in the paint order.

- `trackId` (string, required)
- `toIndex` (integer, required)

## updateProject

Update project settings (name, dimensions, fps).

- `name` (string, optional)
- `width` (integer, optional)
- `height` (integer, optional)
- `fps` (number, optional)

## addAsset

Register a media asset (video, audio, or image) for use by elements.

- `asset` (object, required)

## updateAsset

Patch asset metadata (e.g. probed duration or dimensions).

- `assetId` (string, required)
- `patch` (object, required)

## removeAsset

Remove an asset and every element that references it.

- `assetId` (string, required)

## addElement

Add an element to a track. Elements on a track may not overlap in time. editMode "normal" (default) rejects collisions; "overwrite" clears the landing range; "insert" splits at the point and ripples everything after it right on every unlocked track. Omit `element.id` to have one generated.

- `trackId` (string, required)
- `element` (object, required)
- `editMode` ("normal" | "overwrite" | "insert", optional)

## removeElement

Remove an element from the timeline.

- `elementId` (string, required)

## moveElement

Move an element in time and optionally to another track. editMode "normal" (default) rejects collisions; "overwrite" clears the landing range; "insert" ripples clips after the landing point right.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `startMs` (integer, required)
- `toTrackId` (string, optional)
- `editMode` ("normal" | "overwrite" | "insert", optional)

## trimElement

Set element timing. `startMs`/`durationMs` position it on the timeline; `trimStartMs` (video/audio) offsets into the source media.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `startMs` (integer, optional)
- `durationMs` (integer, optional)
- `trimStartMs` (integer, optional)

## splitElement

Split an element at an absolute timeline time into two elements.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `atMs` (integer, required)
- `rightElementId` (string, optional)

## updateElement

Patch element properties (text, style, transform, opacity, volume, ...). The merged element is re-validated; `id` and `type` cannot change.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `patch` (object, required)

## applyCaptions

Add caption elements (e.g. from a transcription) to a caption track, creating the track when needed.

- `trackId` (string, optional)
- `replace` (boolean, optional)
- `captions` (object[], required)

## setKeyframe

Add or update a keyframe on a fixed-effect property (position.x/y, scale.x/y, rotation, opacity, blur, volume). The first keyframe arms the property (stopwatch on): an armed property is driven entirely by its keyframes. `timeMs` is element-local (0 = clip start). `easing` shapes the curve TOWARD the next keyframe.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `property` (enum, required)
- `timeMs` (integer, required)
- `value` (number, required)
- `easing` ("linear" | "hold" | "easeIn" | "easeOut" | "easeInOut" | object, optional)

## removeKeyframe

Remove the keyframe at an exact element-local time. Removing the last one disarms the property.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `property` (enum, required)
- `timeMs` (integer, required)

## moveKeyframe

Retime a keyframe (drag a diamond), preserving its value and easing.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `property` (enum, required)
- `fromTimeMs` (integer, required)
- `toTimeMs` (integer, required)

## setKeyframeEasing

Set temporal interpolation toward the next keyframe: linear, hold, easeIn, easeOut, easeInOut, or { cubicBezier: [x1, y1, x2, y2] }.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `property` (enum, required)
- `timeMs` (integer, required)
- `easing` ("linear" | "hold" | "easeIn" | "easeOut" | "easeInOut" | object, required)

## clearKeyframes

Remove all keyframes for one property (stopwatch off) or for the whole element. The static value takes over again.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `property` (enum, optional)

## applyAnimationPreset

Apply an animation preset that EXPANDS into editable keyframes, built on pro easing curves (expo/quint settles, soft overshoot, M3 exits). In: fade-in, slide-in, pop-in, scale-in, zoom-in, whip-in, blur-in. Out: fade-out, slide-out, pop-out, zoom-out, whip-out, blur-out. Emphasis (whole clip): ken-burns, punch-zoom, pulse, breathe, float, sway, shake. Fast presets (whip-in/out, punch-zoom) also enable per-element motion blur.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `preset` (enum, required)
- `options` (object, optional)

## addEffect

Append a visual effect to an element's effect stack (or insert at `index`). Effects compile to a canvas filter and apply in stack order. Types: blur, brightness, contrast, saturate, grayscale, sepia, hue-rotate, invert, drop-shadow, css (raw CSS filter escape hatch).

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `effect` (object, required)
- `index` (integer, optional)

## updateEffect

Patch the parameters of the effect at `index` in an element's stack (e.g. { radius: 12 } or { enabled: false }). The effect's `type` cannot change.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `index` (integer, required)
- `patch` (object, required)

## removeEffect

Remove the effect at `index` from an element's effect stack.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `index` (integer, required)

## reorderEffect

Move an effect within an element's stack (stack order = apply order).

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `fromIndex` (integer, required)
- `toIndex` (integer, required)

## setBlendMode

Set how a visual element composites against the layers below (multiply, screen, overlay, ...). Pass null for normal.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `blendMode` (enum | null, required)

## setMotionBlur

Set per-element motion blur (After Effects layer model: sub-frame transform samples accumulated across a shutter window). Only KEYFRAMED position/scale/rotation motion blurs. shutterAngle 360 = full frame interval; 180 = film look (default). Pass null to turn it off.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `motionBlur` (object | null, required)

## setTransition

Set (or clear with null) the transition from an element into the NEXT exactly-adjacent clip on the same track. Types: dissolve, fade-black, fade-white, slide-left, slide-right, wipe-left, wipe-right. The blend window is centered on the cut and never longer than either clip; where a clip has no media handles the renderer freezes its boundary frame.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `transition` (object | null, required)

## setElementSpeed

Set a constant playback speed on a video/audio element (2 = twice as fast). The clip keeps its in-point; its timeline duration rescales to play the same source span. Replaces any existing speed ramp with a constant map; speed 1 removes the map. For ramps and freeze-frames use setTimeMap.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `speed` (number, required)

## setTimeMap

Set or clear a time remap curve on a video/audio element: keyframes from element-local output ms to source ms (relative to trimStartMs), monotone non-decreasing. Bezier easing between keyframes = speed ramp; a flat segment = freeze-frame. Pass null to restore 1x.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `timeMap` (object[] | null, required)

## trimEdge

Trim ONE edge of a clip while its content stays anchored: reversed spans, speed maps, keyframes, and caption words all keep showing the same frames. Positive deltaMs moves the edge later. The other edge and every other clip stay put. Prefer this over raw trimElement for edge drags — trimElement edits the source window directly and shifts a reversed clip's content.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `edge` ("start" | "end", required)
- `deltaMs` (integer, required)

## slipElement

Slip a clip: shift WHICH part of the source plays without moving the clip on the timeline. Positive deltaMs slides the source window later. Applies to video/audio (trim offset) and multicam (every source in sync).

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `deltaMs` (integer, required)

## rollEdit

Roll the cut between a clip and its exactly-adjacent NEXT clip: the boundary moves by deltaMs, one clip revealing source while the other conceals it. Track length and every other clip stay put.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `deltaMs` (integer, required)

## slideElement

Slide a clip along its exactly-adjacent neighbors: the clip moves by deltaMs keeping its content; the left neighbor's end and the right neighbor's start absorb the change. Use moveElement across gaps.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `deltaMs` (integer, required)

## rippleTrim

Trim a clip edge AND ripple: everything downstream of the edit shifts by the same amount, so no gap opens or closes unevenly. scope "timeline" (default) shifts every unlocked track; "track" only the clip's own track.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `edge` ("start" | "end", required)
- `deltaMs` (integer, required)
- `scope` ("track" | "timeline", optional)

## saveLayout

Add or replace a multicam layout in the project (slots position sources on the canvas in normalized 0..1 rects; first slot paints bottom).

- `layout` (object, required)

## removeLayout

Remove a project layout. Fails while any multicam cut still uses it.

- `layoutId` (string, required)

## savePreset

Add or replace a property preset: a named bundle of inspector values (an effects stack, a text style, a layout-slot style, …). The preset kind names the surface that captures and applies it.

- `preset` (object, required)

## removePreset

Remove a property preset from the project.

- `presetId` (string, required)

## createMulticam

Combine 1+ video elements into one multicam clip: sources are synced by their current timeline alignment, originals are removed, and the project is seeded with default talking-head layouts (screen + camera) when it has none. Source keys: with two sources the bottom layer becomes "screen" and the top layer "camera" (roles can be reassigned afterwards); audio follows the camera.

- `elementIds` (string[], required)
- `multicamId` (string, optional)

## addAngleCut

Cut a multicam to a layout at an element-local time: the layout is active from `atMs` until the next cut (the live-switching primitive — press a layout key while playing).

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `atMs` (integer, required)
- `layoutId` (string, required)

## moveAngleCut

Retime a multicam cut (drag its tick). Clamped between its neighbors.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `fromMs` (integer, required)
- `toMs` (integer, required)

## removeAngleCut

Remove a multicam cut; the previous layout extends over its span.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `atMs` (integer, required)

## setAngleLayout

Change which layout a multicam span uses without cutting (the paused "correct this take" action; `atMs` is the span's cut time).

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `atMs` (integer, required)
- `layoutId` (string, required)

## setMulticamAudio

Choose which multicam source supplies the audio (null mutes all sources).

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `sourceKey` (string | null, required)

## setMulticamSourceTrim

Nudge one multicam source's sync: its media time at the multicam's start (ms).

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `sourceKey` (string, required)
- `trimStartMs` (integer, required)

## setMulticamAngleTransition

Standardize the cut style of a multicam: one transition blended at EVERY angle cut (null = hard jump cuts). Same vocabulary as clip transitions (dissolve, fade-black, …); each window is centered on its cut and clamped so neighboring windows never overlap.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `transition` (object | null, required)

## setMulticamSourceKey

Reassign a multicam source's role key ('screen', 'camera', …) — the key layout slots match on. If another source already holds `newKey` the two swap keys (audio stays with its role); on a plain rename the audio source follows the renamed key.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `sourceKey` (string, required)
- `newKey` (string, required)

## flattenMulticam

Explode a multicam into plain clips: one video element per cut-span slot (on new tracks, layout geometry baked into transforms — approximate, no crop primitive) plus one audio element from the audio source. One-way; undo restores the multicam.

- `elementId` (string, required)

## detachAudio

Detach a video element's audio onto its own audio element. The video is muted, volume keyframes move to the new audio element, and both share a `linkId` so UIs can select/move them together. Creates a track for the audio when `toTrackId` is omitted.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `toTrackId` (string, optional)
- `audioElementId` (string, optional)

## applyZoomPreset

Apply a saved zoom (relative keyframe pattern: scale multipliers + position deltas) to an element at an element-local time. Expands into editable keyframes; existing keyframes inside the window are replaced. Override durationMs to retime the move.

- `elementId` (string, required)
- `preset` (object, required)
- `atMs` (integer, required)
- `durationMs` (integer, optional)

## addMarker

Add a timeline marker at an absolute time. Omit `id` to have one generated.

- `id` (string, optional)
- `timeMs` (integer, required)
- `label` (string, optional)
- `color` (string, optional)

## updateMarker

Retime, relabel, or recolor a timeline marker.

- `markerId` (string, required)
- `timeMs` (integer, optional)
- `label` (string | null, optional)
- `color` (string | null, optional)

## removeMarker

Remove a timeline marker.

- `markerId` (string, required)

## rippleDelete

Remove elements AND close the gaps they leave: later clips on the same track shift left by the removed duration (Premiere ripple delete). Other tracks are not affected.

- `elementIds` (string[], required)
